L1.area: Database connectivity and storage plugin layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to open and read arbitrary database files on the host system by supplying a crafted relative path containing directory traversal sequences in a database connection request. The user-controlled database name is joined with a default directory path without verifying that the resolved path remains within the intended directory, enabling access to any compatible database file accessible to the application process.

L3.area: Sqlite3 database plugin connection and file resolution logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to open and read arbitrary Sqlite3 database files on the host system by supplying a crafted relative path containing directory traversal sequences when connecting to a Sqlite3 database. The user-controlled database name is joined with a default directory path without verifying that the resolved path remains within the intended directory, enabling access to any Sqlite3 database file accessible to the application process.

ground_truth.reason: The DB function joins a user-controlled database name with a default directory path without validating that the resolved path stays within the intended directory, allowing path traversal via sequences like '../' to access arbitrary Sqlite3 databases on the host.
